content_description: Educational/instructional presentation on surgical training principles, skill acquisition theory, and endoscopic education strategy. Content includes slides on andragogy, Kopta/Fitts skill acquisition stages, learning tools (FES progression model), and institutional training strategy for endoscopic procedures.
presentation_topics: Role of video, simulation, and expert feedback in learning
Tools specific to learning stages (FES progression)
Multi-year training strategy for endoscopy residents
reason_no_score: This is an educational lecture/presentation with no actual FLS task performance visible. No trainee is performing any of the five FLS tasks (peg transfer, pattern cut, endoloop, extracorporeal suture, intracorporeal suture). Content is purely instructional/theoretical.

verbal_cues_inferred: Andragogy emphasizes relevance, immediacy, participation, feedback, and safe learning environment
Motor skill development progresses through cognitive → integrative → autonomous stages
Learner articulation and reflection correlate with high performance
Practice should require reconstruction (varied order), not repetition of same sequence
Focused attention critical; avoid fatigue (morning, 4-hour sessions recommended)
Video superior to books if expert-curated; technology augments but does not replace expert feedback
common_errors_addressed: Excessive time or performance pressure in training
Training not immediately applicable to practice
Overly diverse demonstrations early in learning

confidence_rationale: Video content is unambiguously an educational presentation with slides, no FLS task performance visible.
